categories.summary: This Discord chat segment shows minimal technical discussion, primarily consisting of introductions and casual conversation. The most significant technical contribution came from carlgatz, who described building a local gRPC pipeline to optimize AI token processing during high-volume spikes. The solution involves filtering data before AI processing, keeping fast operations in RAM to avoid token costs, and only using MCP for deep analysis, achieving sub-millisecond latency. Carlgatz is seeking 5 developers to stress test the system and plans to share architecture details. Two users (rsn6958 and neural.x) posted detailed introductions highlighting their AI/ML and full-stack development expertise, focusing on RAG systems, AI agents, voice assistants, and SaaS platforms. The chat also included leaderboard competition discussion between magicyte and valleybeyond7991, with odilitime commenting on the need for harder trivia questions. No concrete implementations were shared beyond carlgatz's high-level architecture description.

faqs.question: Are you looking for developers to help with AI projects?
faqs.answeredBy: carlgatz - seeking 5 devs for stress testing gRPC system; rsn6958 and neural.x offering AI/ML development services

actionItems.description: Stress test local gRPC pipeline for AI token processing optimization with 5 developers
Spin up cloud nodes for gRPC-based AI processing system
Share gRPC architecture and stress test findings with community
Implement harder trivia questions for the platform

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment from the partners channel shows demian_dappcraft reaching out to partners with a proposal. The interaction is brief, with demian_dappcraft initially pinging the partners role seeking attention for an opportunity. odilitime responds positively, offering to review details. demian_dappcraft then sends information via direct message and expresses strong enthusiasm about connecting two products they are passionate about. The conversation represents an initial partnership inquiry rather than a technical discussion. No technical implementations, solutions, or concrete decisions were made in this segment. The exchange is primarily about establishing communication for a potential collaboration between two products, with the substantive discussion deferred to private messages.

helpInteractions.context: demian_dappcraft was seeking attention from partners for a proposal or opportunity
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime agreed to look at the details, and demian_dappcraft sent information via DM

actionItems.description: Explore potential collaboration between two products that demian_dappcraft is passionate about

categories.summary: The discussion centered on two main topics: technical issues with elizacloud signup and a strategic ecosystem integration proposal. valorenvk reported being unable to sign up for elizacloud, receiving a tenant invitation error ("Tenant 'elizacloud' requires an invitation to join"). odilitime confirmed the issue was escalated to the team and someone is investigating, awaiting confirmation of a fix. The more substantial discussion involved valorenvk's proposal to integrate elizaOS with peaq's machine operating system (peaqOS). The proposal suggests creating a native @elizaos/plugin-peaq to enable over 3 million devices on peaq's network to use elizaOS as their autonomous reasoning and decision-making framework. This would position elizaOS as the primary agentic framework for real-world robots and IoT devices operating on a decentralized, pay-per-task basis. The integration would complement peaq's recent Akash integration for hardware compute, providing a fully decentralized AI backend alternative to centralized providers like OpenAI or Google. The strategic value includes network effects as the machine economy scales and positioning elizaOS in the hardware/physical robotics narrative.

faqs.question: Can I sign up for elizacloud? I'm getting an error that says the tenant requires an invitation to join
Has the team looked into integrating elizaOS with peaq's machine operating system layer (peaqOS)?

helpInteractions.context: valorenvk couldn't sign up for elizacloud due to tenant invitation error
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime confirmed the issue was passed to the team and someone is investigating, waiting for fix confirmation

actionItems.description: Fix elizacloud signup issue - tenant invitation error blocking user registration
Build native @elizaos/plugin-peaq integration to connect elizaOS with peaq's 3M+ device network for autonomous reasoning and decision-making
Position elizaOS as premier agentic framework layer for peaq's decentralized machine operating system and robotics network

categories.summary: The discussion channel showed minimal technical activity, primarily consisting of community concerns and promotional content. The most significant topic was a detailed fact-check posted by bangrrr2914 regarding ElizaOS's financial relationship with Secure Blockchain Dev Corp. This analysis outlined a timeline from October 2025 to May 2026, detailing how Eliza Foundation allocated 750k CAD into a placement alongside 500k CAD from Sebastian Quinn Watson, a core defendant in a NY legal case. The post raised concerns about circular fund flow and potential exit liquidity strategies. Community member zadayos inquired about Shaw's recent activity, bug fixes on Eliza Cloud, and launch plans. A brief exchange occurred between odilitime and valorenvk discussing the presence of scam bots in the channel. User ghss44 questioned whether the team had lost faith or abandoned the project, to which om1d_sa responded by directing them to progress channels. User mfairy posted promotional content about a 24-hour launch collaboration opportunity related to PumpFun and DeFi. Multiple Twitter links were shared by magicyte without context. The overall technical discussion was limited, with most substantive content focused on financial transparency concerns rather than development progress.

faqs.question: Is Shaw doing okay? I haven't seen him commit any new code recently. Are the bugs on Eliza Cloud about to be fixed? Will it affect the launch plan? Have you guys run a small-scale pilot yet?
Why is there so many scam bots here?
Did you guys lose faith in the project? Or kind of abandoned it?

helpInteractions.context: User questioned whether the team had abandoned the project
helpInteractions.resolution: Directed to daily progress channels for updates on ongoing development

actionItems.description: Fix bugs on Eliza Cloud platform
Review and verify financial flow structure regarding Secure Blockchain Dev Corp investment and circular fund flow concerns

categories.summary: The discussion centered on two main technical topics: world models implementation and ElizaOS version management. wonderwomancode explained that proper implementation requires world models, recommending Google's Vertex with the Genie model as an immediate solution. They're developing tooling for ElizaOS to make this more accessible, expected in about a month, with plans for migration guides and agent tools. The best results come from fine-tuned world models specific to the use case. om1d_sa encountered version update issues, stuck on ElizaOS v1.7.2 despite attempting updates and reinstallation. odilitime provided the solution: use the develop branch for 2.x versions, which is the current standard practice among most developers rather than waiting for stable releases on the main branch.

faqs.question: How can I update ElizaOS from v1.7.2 to v2 branch?
Should I move to alpha branch for v2 update or wait for stable update on normal branch?

helpInteractions.context: Inquiry about proper implementation approach for a project
helpInteractions.resolution: Recommended world models using Google Vertex with Genie model, mentioned upcoming ElizaOS tooling in a month with migration guides

helpInteractions.context: Unable to update ElizaOS from v1.7.2 to v2 despite reinstallation attempts
helpInteractions.resolution: Advised to use develop branch for 2.x versions as that's what most developers are using

actionItems.description: Develop tooling for ElizaOS to easily spin up world model projects (estimated one month)
Create migration guides for developers building on Vertex to transition to new ElizaOS world model tooling
Develop agent tool for world model integration with ElizaOS

categories.summary: The discussion channel showed minimal technical activity with primarily support requests and status inquiries. The main technical issue reported was a white/blank screen problem on Windows when running 'bun run dev:ui' - the agent starts successfully and skills sync, but the browser displays only a blank screen despite cache clearing, hard refresh attempts, and testing across different browsers. Multiple users reported access issues with elizaCloud, specifically receiving tenant invitation requirement errors when attempting to join. One user inquired about bridging elizaOS from BNB to Solana but received no technical response. There were mentions of various platform access issues including Babylon (confirmed on hold by odilitime, with plans to revive the social media component after discussion with Shaw), and Milady (users unable to access elizaCloud through it or modify OpenRouter API settings). A user asked about Milady development updates, noting GitHub activity has been silent. No concrete solutions were provided for the reported technical issues during this chat segment.

faqs.question: White/blank screen problem on Windows - bun run dev:ui starts, agent ready, skills synced, but browser shows only white screen. Tried cache clear, hard refresh, different browsers. Anyone had this issue?
What is the best way to bridge elizaOS from BNB to Solana?
Any Milady update? Github is silent

helpInteractions.context: User inquiring about Babylon platform status and access issues
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed Babylon is on hold, mentioned discussion with Shaw about reviving the social media part

actionItems.description: Fix white/blank screen issue on Windows when running bun run dev:ui despite successful agent start and skill sync
Resolve elizaCloud tenant invitation requirement error blocking user access
Fix Milady platform access issues preventing elizaCloud connection and OpenRouter API modification
Implement or document bridging solution for elizaOS from BNB to Solana
Resume development and provide updates on Milady project (GitHub activity silent)

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment from the #💬-coders channel contains no technical discussions, development activities, or coding-related content. The conversation consists entirely of three unrelated messages: a general greeting from joei4928, a partnership inquiry from mahe7754, and a solicitation from lileky_07844 regarding purchasing cryptocurrency wallets with transaction history. No programming problems were discussed, no solutions were implemented, and no technical decisions were made. The channel appears to have been used for non-technical purposes during this time period, with messages that are more appropriate for business development or trading channels rather than a coders-focused discussion space.

The discussion channel covered several key topics. The primary technical update was odilitime confirming that all work has been consolidated into the eliza repository. A community member (ryan067828) encountered a scam attempt regarding ai16z token migration, which was quickly identified by the community (paolin_62616). Ryan confirmed the official migration window has closed with no legitimate links available. Saulodigital presented a significant collaboration opportunity involving Semantic Delegation with the ERC-7710 Delegation Framework and Intuition Knowledge Graph, offering $7,500 USDC across 5 missions. The missions include: M09 Tutorial ($1,000), M10 Liquid Democracy POC ($1,000), M11 Liquid Democracy Production ($3,000), M12 Intuition Skill Delegation ($500), and M13 Caveat Enforcers Registry ($2,000). The framework combines authority and meaning layers to enable previously impossible application designs. Saulodigital expressed interest in collaborating with elizaOS. Odilitime directed mahe7754 to DM for proposal submission after automod issues prevented direct posting.

faqs.question: Anything we can play with now?
Can someone help me on ai16z to new token migration?
How can I send my proposal?

helpInteractions.context: ryan067828 received a scam message about ai16z token migration with suspicious wallet connection instructions
helpInteractions.resolution: paolin_62616 identified it as a scam, preventing potential fraud

helpInteractions.context: mahe7754 needed to submit a proposal but didn't know the process
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime instructed to send via DM

helpInteractions.context: saulodigital's post was removed by automod
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime suggested posting in chunks to bypass automod

actionItems.description: Consolidate all work into eliza repository (completed)
Implement ERC-7710 Delegation Framework integration with Intuition Knowledge Graph

M10 Mission: Build Liquid Democracy POC with Graph Domain Caveat Enforcer and 3-tier chain

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment shows minimal technical discussion or development activity. The conversation consists primarily of casual greetings and brief exchanges. One user (rsn6958) inquired about developer opportunities, and another user (ross.ross.ross) asked about ordering humanoid robots, referencing a news article with a dead link mentioning a quantity of 420,000 units. The only substantive response came from odilitime, who indicated that no active hiring is occurring at the moment and mentioned that a couple of project ideas had been started but ultimately fell apart. The rest of the chat contains social media links, greetings (GM/Gm), and casual interactions without technical substance or problem-solving discussions.

faqs.question: Is there anyone looking for a developer?
Are you guys still taking orders for the humanoid robots?

categories.summary: The discussion channel featured limited technical content, primarily consisting of greetings and off-topic messages. The most substantial discussion came from ghss44, who questioned ElizaOS's open-source strategy in the competitive 2026 AI landscape. They argued that open-sourcing everything may cause the project to lag behind competitors, suggesting monetization of latest features while open-sourcing older versions (similar to Elon Musk's approach with Grok). They expressed concern about lack of clear direction and resources, mentioning potential funding issues after migration due to a16z lawsuit threats. ivgamer3467 countered by defending the open-source approach, explaining that it accelerates progress through community contributions, bug fixes, and feedback. They referenced a partnership between Secure Blockchain Development Corp. and the Eliza Foundation to commercialize enterprise-level AI agents built on the open-source elizaOS framework. ryan067828 inquired about missed AI16Z to ELIZAOS token migration, asking if alternative processes exist for late migrations. The conversation also included brief exchanges about Federal Reserve decisions (matthib predicted no change) and spam-related questions. Overall, the channel showed minimal technical problem-solving or concrete development discussions, with most activity being casual conversation and one user attempting to purchase old wallets.

faqs.question: Are you sure elizaOS team that open source is still the way to go with elizaOS in the competitive 2026 AI era?
Is there any alternative process for migrating legacy AI16Z tokens after missing the official deadline?
What's the fed decision going to be?
What is the commercialization partnership between Secure Blockchain Development Corp. and E. Foundation about?

helpInteractions.context: ghss44 questioned the open-source strategy and expressed concerns about lack of direction and lagging behind competitors
helpInteractions.resolution: Explained benefits of open-source including community contributions, bug fixes, and feedback. Referenced partnership for commercializing enterprise-level AI agents on elizaOS framework

actionItems.description: Clarify the partnership details between Secure Blockchain Development Corp. and Eliza Foundation for enterprise AI agent commercialization
Provide clear information about token migration alternatives or finality for users who missed the deadline
Address funding situation and clarify status after migration regarding a16z lawsuit threats

The conversation highlights an ongoing moderation challenge with scammers posting in the channel. No technical development, coding problems, or implementation discussions occurred during this segment.
